response:
*KEEP THE AREA CLEAN*  
BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY AT LEAST TWICE A DAY WITH A SOFT-BRISTLED TOOTHBRUSH.  
RINSE YOUR MOUTH WITH WATER AFTER EVERY MEAL.  
FLOSS CAREFULLY AROUND THE AFFECTED TOOTH.  
*FLOSS CAREFULLY*  
GENTLY REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES TO PREVENT DAMAGE.  
AVOID HARD, STICKY, OR CHEWEY FOODS.  
*AVOID HARD, STICKY, OR CHEWEY FOODS*  
USE TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS FROM PHARMACIES.  
THIS CAN TEMPORARILY FILL THE VOID AND PROTECT THE TOOTH.  
*USE TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS*  
MANAGE PAIN WITH OVER-THE-COUNTER PAIN RELIEVERS.  
USE IBUPROFEN OR ACETAMINOPHEN AS INSTRUCTIONS SAY.  
*MANAGE PAIN WITH OVER-THE-COUNTER PAIN RELIEVERS*  
RINSE WITH SALT WATER TO REDUCE INFLAMMATION AND BACTERIA.  
MIX 1/2 TEASPOON OF SALT IN 8 OUNCES OF WARM WATER.  
*RINSE WITH SALT WATER*  
CHEW ON THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF YOUR MOUTH TO AVOID PRESSURE.  
THIS REDUCES PAIN AND PREVENTS DAMAGE.  
*CHEW ON THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF YOUR MOUTH*  
MONITOR FOR CHANGES IN THE TOOTH.  
WATCH FOR INFLAMMATION, SENSITIVITY, OR INFECTION.  
*MONITOR FOR CHANGES IN THE TOOTH*  
SEE DENTAL CARE AS SOON AS POSSIBLE.  
THIS PREVENTS SEVERE PROBLEMS AND PROMOTES RECOVERY.  
*SEE DENTAL CARE AS SOON AS POSSIBLE*  
DENTAL EMERGENCIES REQUIRE IMMEDIATE ATTENTION.  
SALVAGE THE TOOTH AND PREVENT INFECTION.  
*DENTAL EMERGENCIES REQUIRE IMMEDIATE ATTENTION*  

* * *  
*KEEP THE AREA CLEAN*  
BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY AT LEAST TWICE A DAY WITH A SOFT-BRISTLED TOOTHBRUSH.  
RINSE YOUR MOUTH WITH WATER AFTER EVERY MEAL.
